title: Fix Devin Resume/Retry Logic with Conversation Accumulation

story.i_want: Implement conversation accumulation pattern for retry logic when Devin session resumption is not viable
story.so_that: Failed verification steps can be retried with full conversation context, allowing agents to see and fix previous errors

journey.before: Step execution begins with original prompt sent to Devin agent
journey.during: Agent produces output, verification script runs and may fail, system needs to retry with context
journey.after: Retry completes with accumulated conversation history or succeeds

capability_breakdown.core_action: Capture step execution state and compose retry prompts with full conversation history
inputs: Verification script output from previous attempts
Node name and saga ID for directory structure
outputs: Clear attempt tracking in directory structure
Logs showing retry attempts and accumulated prompt sizes

acceptance_criteria.criterion: Each step execution creates a node-specific directory
acceptance_criteria.gherkin: Given a saga step execution, When the step completes, Then a node-specific directory is created at .sagas/<saga-id>/<node-name>/attempt_<N>/
dependencies: Directory structure must be created before files are written

acceptance_criteria.criterion: Three files captured per attempt: input.txt, output.txt, verification.txt
acceptance_criteria.gherkin: Given a step execution, When state is captured, Then input.txt, output.txt, and verification.txt are written to the attempt directory

acceptance_criteria.criterion: Retry attempts compose accumulated conversation history
acceptance_criteria.gherkin: Given a failed verification, When a retry is triggered, Then the new prompt contains original input + all previous outputs + all previous verification feedback
dependencies: Previous attempt files must be readable

acceptance_criteria.criterion: Each retry creates a new Devin agent (no session resumption)
acceptance_criteria.gherkin: Given a retry attempt, When the orchestrator executes the step, Then a new Devin agent is created (not resuming a previous session)
dependencies: Session resumption logic must be removed

acceptance_criteria.criterion: Retry limit enforced via existing traversal limit mechanism
acceptance_criteria.gherkin: Given a step with retry attempts, When the traversal limit is reached, Then no further retries are attempted
dependencies: Existing traversal limit mechanism must be in place

acceptance_criteria.criterion: File naming is clear and self-documenting
acceptance_criteria.gherkin: Given state files, When examined, Then file names (input.txt, output.txt, verification.txt) clearly indicate their purpose

acceptance_criteria.criterion: Existing saga execution flow preserved (no breaking changes)
acceptance_criteria.gherkin: Given existing sagas, When executed with new code, Then they continue to work without modification
dependencies: All new code must be additive

acceptance_criteria.criterion: Logs clearly show retry attempts and accumulated prompt sizes
acceptance_criteria.gherkin: Given a retry attempt, When logs are examined, Then they show attempt number and accumulated prompt size
dependencies: Logging must be added to retry logic

edge_cases.criterion: First attempt (no previous attempts to accumulate)
edge_cases.gherkin: Given a step execution on first attempt, When state is captured, Then input.txt contains original prompt only
edge_cases.jtbd: Handle first attempt without previous context

edge_cases.gherkin: Given accumulated prompt exceeds token limits, When retry is attempted, Then agent returns error and verification fails
Given verification script runs successfully with no output, When state is captured, Then verification.txt is empty or contains success indicator

edge_cases.criterion: Node directory already exists from previous saga run
edge_cases.gherkin: Given node directory exists from previous saga, When new saga runs same node, Then new attempt subdirectory is created with incremented number
Given attempt subdirectory exists but files are incomplete, When retry logic checks for next attempt, Then next attempt number is correctly determined

edge_cases.criterion: Multiple retries with growing prompt size
edge_cases.gherkin: Given 5+ retry attempts, When accumulated prompt is composed, Then all previous outputs and verification feedback are included

open_questions.question: Should we implement prompt compression/summarization to reduce token usage?
open_questions.answer: No, not in initial implementation. Keep it simple.

open_questions.question: What happens if accumulated prompt exceeds token limits?
open_questions.answer: Let it fail naturally. Agent will return error, verification will fail, saga will exit.

open_questions.question: Should we preserve old attempt directories or clean them up on success?
open_questions.answer: Preserve all attempts for debugging. Clean up on saga completion (future enhancement).

open_questions.question: Where should compose_retry_prompt() live - DevinWrapper or Orchestrator?
open_questions.answer: Orchestrator - it's orchestration logic, not agent-specific.

existing_code: orchestrator/devin_wrapper.py - DevinWrapper class for agent invocation
Existing traversal limit mechanism for retry enforcement
new_code: Orchestrator.compose_retry_prompt() - Load previous attempts and build accumulated conversation
Modified Orchestrator._write_session_state() - Create node directories and write three files
Modified SagaExecutor._execute_step() - Detect retries and call compose_retry_prompt()
infrastructure: File system for state directory structure

technical_knowledge: Python file I/O and directory operations
Devin CLI invocation and output handling

complexity.rationale: Implementation is straightforward (file I/O and prompt composition), but touches critical retry logic. Risk is medium due to impact on saga execution flow. Uncertainty is low because requirements are well-defined.

recommendation: Proceed with implementation. Requirements are clear and well-documented. The conversation accumulation pattern is a pragmatic solution to the Devin CLI session ID limitation. Implement in three phases: (1) State Capture, (2) Prompt Composition, (3) Integration. Start with Phase 1 to establish the file structure, then Phase 2 for prompt composition, and finally Phase 3 to integrate retry detection into SagaExecutor. All changes are additive and maintain backward compatibility.
